title: Dolichos formosanus
taxonomy: Dolichos formosanus is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Taiwan and is a type of climbing vine. I have found that the plant is classified as a perennial vine and is a member of the genus Dolichos. The specific classification of the plant is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, order Fabales, family Fabaceae, genus Dolichos, species D. formosanus. Further classification includes the plant being in the subfamily Faboideae.

preparations: Dolichos formosanus, a plant native to Taiwan, has been used in traditional herbal preparations for various purposes. intro: Dolichos formosanus, also known as Formosan Dolichos or Formosan bean, is a perennial leguminous vine native to Taiwan, belonging to the genus Dolichos. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of various diseases and promote overall health. The bioactive constituents of Dolichos formosanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dolichos formosanus is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into supplements and other herbal preparations.
